title: Form a view on the target's AI-execution capability
guidance: Reward a stated, calibrated view with a named flip condition. Penalise synthesis with no view or false confidence.

parts.label: Flip condition
parts.note: the item that would change it

runner_input.instruction: Give a red/amber/green view on AI-execution capability, with rationale and the diligence item that would flip it.
runner_input.constraints: State the colour. Two-line rationale. Name one item that would change the rating.

source_blocks.text: CTO Daniel Mertens is praised across primary interviews and the team has shipped real AI, but the CIM org chart names 11 developers, 1 DevOps, 2 product and 3 QA across an 18-FTE engineering team — and no ML or data engineer. AutoSchedule's accuracy implies real ML plumbing exists; whether it is built in-house, rented from a vendor, or contractor-delivered is unknown.

items: AutoSchedule (AI asset/process-to-schedule mapping) shipped Jan 2026; £140k ARR by month 4; 88–95% accuracy on a 12,000-SKU test
Proprietary AI authoring tool used by the 8-FTE food-safety author team; an analytics module was pulled forward
Opportunity levers: 40–55% authorship efficiency; a compliance-scoring layer at 2–3x ARPU; an SME tier for ~120,000 small food businesses priced out today

source_blocks.text: No written AI roadmap has been shared. No feature store, vector DB or AI observability is mentioned. AI training and upskilling are referenced; no AI-specific hiring plan exists.

rubric_notes: The defensible call is amber: real shipped AI but no visible ML ownership.
The flip item is the management session confirming dedicated ML headcount or a named MLOps partner plus a costed roadmap.
